label: tilt orbits to camera + N-body gravitational physics
notes: Two-part move addressing: (1) the prior 3 perpendicular orbits had 2 of 3 in planes the camera was edge-on to — they read as 1D oscillations. Rewrote orbit equations to tilt those planes 60° so all 3 project as visible ellipses. (2) Replaced kinematic orbits with stateful N-body gravitational simulation: pairwise softened gravity (F = G·m·dr / (|dr|² + ε²)^1.5), central restoring force, velocity damping, smooth pull-back beyond escape_r, Velocity Verlet integration sub-stepped 4x per frame. State (positions + velocities) persists in pos_motes.storage['state'] between cooks. Initial conditions seed from the tilted-orbit positions/velocities so the first physics frame is identical to the prior clockwork — then the bodies start interacting. Added ctrl_motes constantCHOP (10 channels: G, mass0/1/2, softening, restoring_k, damping, substeps, escape_r, pullback_k) wired into pos_motes input[1] for live tuning.

operations.post_state: constantCHOP, 10 channels: G=0.05, mass0=1, mass1=1, mass2=1, softening=0.05, restoring_k=0.30, damping=0.02, substeps=4, escape_r=2.0, pullback_k=1.5

operations.before_summary: stateless 3 perpendicular-plane circular orbits (XY, YZ, XZ) at 0.30/0.40/0.50 Hz, R=0.25, no inter-body interaction. Two of three projected as 1D oscillations from the camera POV.
operations.after_summary: stateful N-body gravity sim with softening + central spring + damping + smooth pull-back; Velocity Verlet, 4x substeps; state on me.storage; seeds from tilted-orbit positions/velocities.
